zh.lifeSummary: 當Samuel Henry Adams於1617年1月出生在英國英格蘭薩默塞特巴頓 聖 大衛時，他的父親Henry Adams34歲，母親Edith Squire11614歲。他於1646年1月31日與Mary Rose Eglesfield在美國麻薩諸塞州素福爾可結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。他於1667年登記服兵役。他於1670年在英國英格蘭素福爾可洛維斯托福特被列為rope maker。他去世於1688年1月24日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼米德萊可傑爾姆斯福德享年71歲，埋葬在美國麻薩諸塞州素福爾可波斯頓哈爾斯托。

nameMeanings.name: Adams
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Dutch, and German (mainly northwestern Germany): patronymic from the personal name Adam . In North America, this surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, e.g. Greek Adamopoulos , Serbian and Croatian Adamović (see Adamovich ), Polish (and Jewish) Adamski .
Irish and Scottish: adopted for McAdam or a Scottish variant of Adam , with excrescent -s.

History: This surname was borne by two early presidents of the US, father and son. They were descended from Henry Adams, who settled in Braintree, MA, in 1635/6, from Barton St. David, Somerset, England. The younger of them, John Quincy Adams (1767–1848) derived his middle name from his maternal grandmother's surname (see   Quincy  ). — Another important New England family, established mainly in NH, is descended from William Adams, who emigrated from Shropshire, England, to Dedham, MA, in 1628. James Hopkins Adams (1812–61), governor of SC, was unconnected with either of these families, his ancestry being Welsh; his forebears entered North America through PA.

variants.name: Adam
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Pasquale, Adriano, Aldo, Amadeo, Amedeo, Antonio, Armando, Cristiano, Donato, Emilio, Erasmo, Eugenio, Gerado, Marco, Salvatore, Saverio.
Italian: patronymic from the personal name Adamo, an equivalent of Adam .

nameMeanings.name: Samuel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name (Hebrew Shemuel), possibly meaning ‘He (God) has hearkened’ (presumably to the prayers of a mother for a son). It may also be understood as a contracted form of Hebrew sha'ulme'el meaning ‘asked of God’. In the case of Samuel the son of Hannah, this would be more in keeping with his mother's statement ‘Because I have asked him of the Lord’ (1 Samuel 1:20). Living in the 11th century bc , Samuel was a Hebrew judge and prophet of the greatest historical importance, who established the Hebrew monarchy, anointing as king both Saul and, later, David. In the Authorized Version two books of the Old Testament are named after him, although in Roman Catholic and Orthodox versions of the Bible they are known as the first and second Book of Kings. The story of Samuel being called by God while still a child serving in the house of Eli the priest (1 Samuel 3) is of great vividness and has moved countless generations. In England and America the name was particularly popular among the 16th-century Puritans and among Nonconformists from the 17th to the 19th century. It became fashionable again in the 1990s.

nameMeanings.name: Henry
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: A perennially popular given name, of Continental Germanic origin, from haim ‘home’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’. It was an Old French name, adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. It has been borne by eight kings of England. Not until the 17th century did the form Henry (as opposed to Harry ) become the standard vernacular form, mainly under the influence of the Latin form Henricus and French Henri.

en.lifeSummary: When Samuel Henry Adams was born in January 1617, in Barton St David, Somerset, England, United Kingdom, his father, Henry Adams, was 34 and his mother, Edith Squire, was 11614. He married Mary Rose Eglesfield on 31 January 1646, in Suffolk, Massachusetts, United States. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. He registered for military service in 1667. In 1670, at the age of 53, his occupation is listed as rope maker in Lowestoft, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom. He died on 24 January 1688, in Chelmsford, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, at the age of 71, and was buried in Charlestown, Boston, Suffolk, Massachusetts, United States.

ko.lifeSummary: Samuel Henry Adams(이)가 1617년 1월 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트 바턴 스트 데이비드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Henry Adams(은)는 34세였고, 그의 어머니 Edith Squire(은)는 11614세였다. 그는 1646년 1월 31일 미국 매사추세츠 주 수폴크에서 Mary Rose Eglesfield(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1667년에 군복무를 위해 등록했다. 1670년, 53세의 나이로 그의 직업은 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 로스토프트에서 rope maker이었다. 그는 1688년 1월 24일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 미들레세스 헬스퍼드에서 71세의 나이로 사망했으며 미국 매사추세츠 주 수폴크 보스턴 찰레스타운에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Samuel Henry Adams nasceu em janeiro de 1617, em Barton St David, Somerset, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Henry Adams, tinha 34 anos e sua mãe, Edith Squire, tinha 11614 anos. Ele casou-se com Mary Rose Eglesfield em 31 de janeiro de 1646, em Suffolk, Massachusetts, Estados Unidos da América. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos. Ele alistou-se no serviço militar em 1667. Em 1670, com 53 anos, sua profissão aparece como rope maker em Lowestoft, Suffolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Ele faleceu em 24 de janeiro de 1688, em Chelmsford, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, com 71 anos, e foi sepultado em Charlestown, Boston, Suffolk, Massachusetts, Estados Unidos da América.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Samuel Henry Adams родился январь 1617 г. в местности бартон ст дэвид, сомирсет, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Henry Adams было 34 года, а матери, которую звали Edith Squire, 11614. Он женился на женщине по имени Mary Rose Eglesfield 31 января 1646 г. в местности суффолк, Массачусетс, Соединенные штаты. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. В 1667 году он поступил на военную службу. В 1670 году, в возрасте 53 лет, работал по профессии rope maker в местности лоуестофт, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания. Он умер 24 января 1688 г. в местности челмсфорд, миддлесекс, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 71 год и был похоронен в местности чарльстоун, бостон, суффолк, Массачусетс, Соединенные штаты.

Se casó con Mary Rose Eglesfield el 31 de enero de 1646, en Suffolk, Masachusets, Estados Unidos. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos. Se enlistó en el ejército en 1667. En 1670, a la edad de 53 años, su ocupación registrada era rope maker en Lowestoft, Suffolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Murió el 24 de enero de 1688, en Chelmsford, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, a la edad de 71 años, y fue sepultado en Charlestown, Boston, Suffolk, Masachusets, Estados Unidos.
